channel.title: NYT > Movies
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: ‘The Super Mario Galaxy Movie’ Review: It’s-a Meh
item.description: The sequel to the mega-blockbuster can’t hold still long enough to let us enjoy the good stuff.
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson

item.title: ‘The Drama’ Review: Her Secret Is His Crisis
item.description: Zendaya and Robert Pattinson play a couple upended when one confesses to coming close to committing a horrific crime.
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis
item.media:description: Robert Pattinson, left, with Zendaya, in their well-appointed Boston apartment in the movie “The Drama.”

item.title: Blake Lively’s Harassment Claims Against Justin Baldoni Dismissed as Case Heads to Trial
item.description: A federal judge tossed the actress’s sexual harassment claims against Justin Baldoni in a split decision that sets the stage for a contentious trial.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs
item.media:description: In a ruling on Thursday, a federal judge allowed only a portion of Blake Lively’s lawsuit against Justin Baldoni to go forward.

item.title: When Stars’ Plastic Surgery Is Played for Your Entertainment
item.description: On TikTok and Instagram, content creators play detective, trying to figure out what work has been done. But they’re really just critiquing women’s looks.
item.dc:creator: Esther Zuckerman

item.title: ‘The Blue Trail’ Review: A Drifting Journey Into Freedom
item.description: In an imaginary Brazil, where older people are forcibly removed from society, a resolute 77-year-old seeks her independence in the Amazon.
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis

item.title: How a Mistake Made ‘Project Hail Mary’s’ Rocky Into a Breakout Star
item.description: Rocky was brought to life through a combination of puppetry and visual effects. But his charming personality was the result of a misunderstanding.
item.dc:creator: Esther Zuckerman

item.title: ‘A Great Awakening’ Review: An Unlikely Founding Friendship
item.description: This film tracks the friendship of Benjamin Franklin and the Rev. George Whitefield, who brought a Protestant revival to the American colonies.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Kenny

item.title: ‘DJ Ahmet’ Review: Finding Hidden Music in the Forest
item.description: After a young shepherd in North Macedonia finds a dance party in the woods, his life changes.
item.dc:creator: Chris Azzopardi

item.title: Three Great Documentaries to Stream
item.description: In this month’s picks, reflections on a comedian couple, a charged reality-TV series and activism in Northern Ireland.
item.dc:creator: Ben Kenigsberg

item.title: ‘The Truth and Tragedy of Moriah Wilson’ Review: Behind a Cyclist’s Death
item.description: A crime documentary on the murder of a professional athlete leans into exploitation.
item.dc:creator: Ben Kenigsberg
item.category: The Truth and Tragedy of Moriah Wilson (Movie)

item.title: ‘Pizza Movie’ Review: Dude, Where’s My Pie?
item.description: After taking psychedelics, college roommates, Jack (Gaten Matarazzo) and Montgomery (Sean Giambrone), experience unique side effects.
item.dc:creator: Calum Marsh

item.title: ‘The Stranger’ Review: An Unexamined Life
item.description: A man of indifference becomes a killer in this adaptation of a 1942 Albert Camus novel.
item.dc:creator: Jeannette Catsoulis

item.title: ‘Living the Land’ Review: Rural China in Transition
item.description: This drama follows a boy of 10, left in the care of relatives when his parents are lured to the city by the prospect of jobs created by a tech revolution.
item.dc:creator: Beatrice Loayza

item.title: ‘Star Spangled to Death’: Ken Jacobs’s History of the United States
item.description: At the Museum of Modern Art through April 7, audiences can enter and exit a screening of the 6½-hour film, which Jacobs began in the 1950s.
item.dc:creator: J. Hoberman

item.title: The Best Movies and TV Shows Streaming in April 2026
item.description: A new Jonathan Glatzer tech satire and an adaptation of Isabel Allende’s novel “The House of the Spirits” are among this month’s highlights.
item.dc:creator: Noel Murray
item.category: The House of the Spirits (TV Program)
Star Wars: Maul - Shadow Lord (TV Program)

item.title: Slava Tsukerman, Who Directed the Cult Classic ‘Liquid Sky,’ Dies at 86
item.description: A Russian-born director, he created a film about New Wave models and killer aliens in 1980s New York, helping to reshape independent filmmaking in America.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

item.title: How Instagram’s ‘PG-13’ Branding for Teens Unraveled
item.description: The social media giant, under legal pressure from the Motion Picture Association, has retreated from its use of the movie rating in its marketing.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs and Callie Holtermann
item.media:credit: Loren Elliott for The New York Times
item.media:description: Instagram introduced Teen Accounts in 2024 in an effort to beef up privacy and address concerns about inappropriate content for teenage users.

item.title: ‘Dog Day Afternoon’ on Broadway, With Jon Bernthal and Ebon Moss-Bachrach, Is Underbaked
item.description: A raucous adaptation of a gritty portrait of New York stifles tension with comedy, leaving its stars, Jon Bernthal and Ebon Moss-Bachrach, adrift.
item.dc:creator: Jason Zinoman
item.media:description: Jon Bernthal, center, as Sonny, with Jessica Hecht as the mouthy bank teller Colleen and Danny Johnson as a security guard in “Dog Day Afternoon” at the August Wilson Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: Mary Beth Hurt, Actress Acclaimed in ‘Interiors’ and ‘Garp,’ Dies at 79
item.description: She elevated supporting film roles with insight and improvisational skill, a talent she took to Broadway, earning Tony nominations.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

item.title: Symphony Space to Undergo a $45 Million Makeover
item.description: The Upper West Side performing arts venue will take its programming across the city while its doors close for a 15-month overhaul.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Towfighi
item.media:description: Symphony Space will return with a signature metal marquee, when renovations are complete.

item.title: With Mario and Minecraft, Hollywood Courts Gamers
item.description: Super Mario Bros. and Minecraft became movie blockbusters, and Call of Duty and Legend of Zelda adaptations are on the way. Fans of the video games are watching closely.
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Morgan
item.media:credit: Warner Bros. Pictures, via Associated Press
item.media:description: “A Minecraft Movie,” which is based on the best-selling video game of all time, made nearly $1 billion at the box office last year.

item.title: Most Stylish Stars of the 2026 Awards Season: Teyana Taylor, Odessa A’Zion and More
item.description: Whether loud or quiet, formal or relaxed, the awards season attire of these eight individuals was surprising, delightful and worn with ease.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi

item.title: James Tolkan, a Tough-Talking Actor in ‘Back to the Future’ and ‘Top Gun,’ Dies at 94
item.description: Mr. Tolkan’s career spanned decades but his breakout roles came as an authority figure in two popular films of the mid-1980s.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Ortiz
item.media:description: As Cmdr. Tom “Stinger” Jardian in “Top Gun,” Mr. Tolkan gives a verbal lashing to young pilots who lacked discipline.

item.title: Jon Bernthal and Ebon Moss-Bachrach on Bringing ‘Dog Day Afternoon’ to Broadway
item.description: Jon Bernthal and Ebon Moss-Bachrach are both making their Broadway debut in a high-stakes adaptation of the beloved 1975 film “Dog Day Afternoon.”
item.dc:creator: Melena Ryzik
item.media:credit: Jonah Rosenberg for The New York Times
item.media:description: “Fear is a good inspiration,” Moss-Bachrach, left, said of the process of creating a living, breathing, sometimes frazzling Broadway production.

item.title: Outside Kennedy Center, Jane Fonda and Joan Baez Raise Voices in Protest
item.description: The pair joined in a gathering of artists and others who denounced censorship and faulted President Trump’s growing influence over the nation’s cultural life.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts
item.media:credit: Alex Kent for The New York Times

item.title: Alexander Kluge, 94, Revolutionary Filmmaker in Postwar Germany, Dies
item.description: As a director, theorist and prolific author, he was one of his country’s towering artists and public intellectuals.
item.dc:creator: A.J. Goldmann

item.title: Stream These Movies and Shows Before They Leave Netflix in April
item.description: Several Christopher Guest mockumentaries and almost every James Bond movie are among the titles leaving for U.S. subscribers before the month ends.
item.dc:creator: Jason Bailey

item.title: Five Horror Movies to Stream Now
item.description: This month brings paranoid psychopaths, ungodly subjugations and fiery suspicions.
item.dc:creator: Erik Piepenburg

item.title: 5 Takeaways From Netflix’s ‘BTS: The Return’ Documentary
item.description: Netflix’s documentary about the superstar K-pop group’s comeback after a four-year hiatus showcases its creative process, brotherhood and anxieties about fame.
item.dc:creator: Regina Kim
item.media:description: From left, the BTS members Jin, Suga, Jimin, V, Jung Kook and RM in a scene from the documentary “BTS: The Return.”

item.title: 9 New Movies Our Critics Are Talking About This Week
item.description: Whether you’re a casual moviegoer or an avid buff, our reviewers think these films are worth knowing about.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
